 {"id":"beads_rust-g3i","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - Project Setup & Core Types","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Goals
Establish scaffolding, core types, schema, and storage primitives needed for all commands.

## Deliverables
- Project scaffolding (`Cargo.toml`, toolchain, layout) with YAML config deps.
- Model types (Issue/Dependency/Comment/Event) matching classic JSON shape.
- ID generation + content hashing (base36 adaptive, child counters).
- SQLite schema compatibility + migrations.
- Storage core with transaction discipline and dirty/event hooks.
- Error handling + output conventions.

## Dependencies
- Must complete before Phase 2 (core commands).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema matches bd (PRAGMA table_info/indexes).
- Unit tests for model validation + hashing.
- Storage CRUD works in memory + file DB.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:10:50.667984683Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:02.794628193Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:57:02.794628193Z","close_reason":"Completed Phase 1: Foundation (scaffolding, models, storage, schema, logging, tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-g3xk","title":"E2E tests: epic command","description":"# E2E Tests for `epic` Command

## Commands to Test
- `br epic status <epic_id>` - Show epic status with children
- `br epic add <epic_id> <issue_id>` - Add issue to epic
- `br epic remove <epic_id> <issue_id>` - Remove issue from epic
- `br epic close-eligible <epic_id>` - Check if epic can be closed

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Create epic, add child issues, verify status
2. Epic status shows child count and completion %
3. close-eligible returns true when all children closed
4. close-eligible returns false with open children
5. Remove child from epic

### Error Cases
6. Epic status on non-epic issue → error
7. Add issue to non-existent epic → error
8. Add epic to itself (cycle) → error

### Edge Cases
9. Nested epics (epic containing epic)
10. Epic with 0 children
11. Epic with 100+ children
12. Auto-close epic when last child closed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_epic.rs
- [ ] 12+ test functions
- [ ] Tests verify parent-child dependency semantics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CedarVale","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:26:04.584308742Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:18:11.572352449Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T17:18:11.572352449Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive E2E test suite for epic command with 12 tests covering: epic status progress tracking, eligibility detection, close-eligible command, dry-run mode, eligible-only filter, childless epics, nested epics, no-epics message, partial progress, closed epics filtering, and dependency removal on delete. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-g5dr","title":"Define MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT const shared between sync and CLI","description":"SUBTASK 3.3: The number 10 is used as a preview limit for displaying missing issue IDs during export safety checks. It appears in TWO modules:

Location 1 — src/sync/mod.rs:1400:
  .len().min(10) and if missing_list.len() > 10 { format!("... and {} more", ...) }

Location 2 — src/cli/commands/sync.rs:512,519:
  .len().min(10) and if missing_list.len() > 10 { ... }

This is cross-module duplication — both the sync engine and the CLI sync command independently limit how many missing IDs to show.

CHANGES:
- Add in sync/mod.rs: pub(crate) const MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT: usize = 10;
- Update sync/mod.rs:1400 to use the const
- Update cli/commands/sync.rs:512,519 to import and use the const from sync module

DESIGN CHOICE: Put the const in sync/mod.rs (not CLI) because the sync engine is the authoritative source for this logic. The CLI is a consumer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:54.764362711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.983304096Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:02:27.982837572Z","close_reason":"MISSING_IDS_PREVIEW_LIMIT pub(crate) const in sync/mod.rs, imported by cli/commands/sync.rs. Cross-module duplication eliminated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-cutting"]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-g6j2","title":"Add freshness checks for agent baseline snapshots","description":"Reality-check finding: CHANGELOG_AGENT_FRIENDLY.md advertises agent_baseline snapshots as static machine-readable artifacts, but agent_baseline/help/br_help.txt has already drifted from current br help ordering/text and does not reflect the latest source help change for --lock-timeout. Stale baselines undermine zero-shot agent onboarding. Acceptance: add a documented regeneration command and CI/test check that compares agent_baseline help/schema/example artifacts against the built binary, or clearly mark them as historical snapshots with version metadata.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:06:49.863386042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:06:49.863386042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-workflow","docs","reality-check"]}

Stale baselines undermine zero-shot agent onboarding. Acceptance: add a documented regeneration command and CI/test check that compares agent_baseline help/schema/example artifacts against the built binary, or clearly mark them as historical snapshots with version metadata.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyDune","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:06:49.863386042Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:26:51.056011936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:26:51.055680364Z","close_reason":"Added an agent_baseline freshness test, documented regeneration, and refreshed current snapshots","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-workflow","docs","reality-check"],"comments":[{"id":282,"issue_id":"beads_rust-g6j2","author":"MistyDune","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail health is red; intended files likely agent_baseline/** plus focused docs/test/check plumbing after inspection. Avoiding Codex4 wf7z files.","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:11:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-gh24","title":"E2E tests: reopen command (expanded)","description":"# E2E Tests for `reopen` Command (Expanded)

## Current State
Reopen has partial E2E coverage. Needs expansion.

## Commands to Test
- `br reopen <id>` - Reopen closed issue
- `br reopen <id> --reason <text>` - Reopen with reason
- `br reopen --json` - JSON output

## Test Cases
### Success Paths
1. Reopen closed issue → status open
2. Reopen with reason, verify in notes/history
3. Reopen deferred issue (undefer)
4. JSON output structure

### Error Cases
5. Reopen already-open issue → error or no-op
6. Reopen non-existent issue → error
7. Reopen tombstone → error or allowed?

### Edge Cases
8. Reopen then close again (cycle)
9. Reopen issue with dependencies still open
10. Reopen preserves all other fields
11. Reopen updates updated_at timestamp

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_reopen.rs (or extend existing)
- [ ] 11+ test functions
- [ ] Verify event log captures reopen event","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:27:40.118077077Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:25.350458001Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:25.350458001Z","close_reason":"E2E reopen tests exist: tests/e2e_changelog.rs has changelog_reopen_then_close test and conformance.rs has conformance_reopen_basic test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-gh24","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-gjgz","title":"Define named display limit constants in mcp/tools.rs","description":"SUBTASK 4.1: src/mcp/tools.rs uses bare .take(N) calls for display truncation in MCP tool responses. The sibling module mcp/prompts.rs already demonstrates the correct pattern with named constants (STATUS_REPORT_LABELS_LIMIT, BOTTLENECK_DISPLAY_LIMIT, etc. at lines 30-34). Apply the same pattern to tools.rs.

SPECIFIC LOCATIONS:
- Line 1012: .take(10) for recent events in show_issue tool response
  Add: const RECENT_EVENTS_DISPLAY_LIMIT: usize = 10;

- Line 1950: .take(15) for top labels in project overview
  Add: const TOP_LABELS_DISPLAY_LIMIT: usize = 15;

- Line 1953: .take(10) for blocked issues in project overview
  Add: const BLOCKED_ISSUES_DISPLAY_LIMIT: usize = 10;

- Line 1960: .take(10) for ready issues in project overview
  Add: const READY_ISSUES_DISPLAY_LIMIT: usize = 10;

NAMING CONVENTION: Follow the prompts.rs pattern — SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE with _DISPLAY_LIMIT or _LIMIT suffix. Group all these constants together near the top of tools.rs, after the existing PLACEHOLDER_EXACT and UPDATE_FIELD_KEYS constants.

REASONING: MCP tool responses have token budgets. These limits control how much data goes into a single tool response. Having them as named constants lets us tune them based on agent feedback about response sizes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:25.065757972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:37.745260174Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:54:37.744753353Z","close_reason":"Added DEFAULT_LIST_LIMIT, MAX_LIST_LIMIT, RECENT_EVENTS_DISPLAY_LIMIT, TOP_LABELS_DISPLAY_LIMIT, BLOCKED_ISSUES_DISPLAY_LIMIT, READY_ISSUES_DISPLAY_LIMIT in mcp/tools.rs. All .take(N) and .unwrap_or(N) replaced.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-gjgz","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ok3g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:56.745586838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-gk8","title":"Storage unit tests: Dependency graph operations","description":"Test add_dependency, remove_dependency, get_dependencies, get_dependents, cycle detection. Test deep hierarchies (5+ levels), diamond patterns (A->B,C->D), blocked cache invalidation on dep change. Real SQLite, no mocks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:30:15.105681617Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:50:48.606528224Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:50:48.606528224Z","close_reason":"All 28 tests pass covering: add/remove_dependency, get_dependencies/dependents, cycle detection, deep hierarchies (5+ levels), diamond patterns, and blocked cache invalidation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

@@ -4,6 +4,23 @@ Goal: validate that a "fresh" agent can use br using only docs + `--help` (no so
 
 Date: 2026-01-25
 
+Freshness: these files are current snapshots, not historical examples. The
+integration test `agent_baseline_snapshots_match_current_binary` compares the
+captured help, schema, example, version, and error artifacts against the built
+`br` binary.
+
+Regenerate after intentional CLI/schema/output changes:
+
+```bash
+UPDATE_AGENT_BASELINE=1 cargo test --test e2e_schema agent_baseline_snapshots_match_current_binary -- --nocapture
+```
+
+Check without rewriting files:
+
+```bash
+cargo test --test e2e_schema agent_baseline_snapshots_match_current_binary
+```
+
 ## Tasks attempted
 
 1. List ready issues in TOON and decode to JSON.

@@ -34,6 +34,14 @@ As a fallback, this repo also includes a captured snapshot bundle under:
 
 - `agent_baseline/schemas/`
 
+Those snapshots are checked against the built binary by the
+`agent_baseline_snapshots_match_current_binary` test. After intentional schema
+changes, regenerate them with:
+
+```bash
+UPDATE_AGENT_BASELINE=1 cargo test --test e2e_schema agent_baseline_snapshots_match_current_binary -- --nocapture
+```
+
 ## Key folding (TOON)
 
 When emitting TOON, br may "fold" nested keys into dotted keys (safe folding) to save tokens.
